```python
class Node:
    """
    A node in the doubly linked list.
    Each node stores a key-value pair and pointers to previous and next nodes.
    """
    def __init__(self, key: int = None, value: int = None):
        self.key = key
        self.value = value
        self.prev = None
        self.next = None


class LRUCache:
    """
    LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache implementation using a doubly linked list and a hash map.
    
    The doubly linked list maintains the order of usage, with the most recently used
    item at the head (right after dummy head) and the least recently used item at the
    tail (right before dummy tail).
    
    The hash map (dictionary) provides O(1) access to nodes by key.
    
    Time Complexity: O(1) for both get and put operations.
    Space Complexity: O(capacity) for storing up to capacity items.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        """
        Initialize the LRU cache with a given capacity.
        
        Args:
            capacity: The maximum number of items the cache can hold.
        """
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = {}  # Hash map: key -> Node
        
        # Create dummy head and tail nodes to simplify edge cases
        # The actual most recently used item is right after head
        # The actual least recently used item is right before tail
        self.head = Node()  # Dummy head
        self.tail = Node()  # Dummy tail
        
        # Link dummy head and tail together
        self.head.next = self.tail
        self.tail.prev = self.head
